questions.question: What new fields does discovery state need?
questions.recommendation: Add answers{}, non_goals[], acceptance_criteria[], unresolved[] to state.json
questions.rationale: These are the structured fields from the I-7 spec; unresolved[] becomes the enforceable gate

questions.question: How does the blocking gate work?
questions.recommendation: unresolved[] being non-empty blocks spec checkpoint; complete flag kept as second axis
questions.rationale: Codex adversarial review confirmed complete is load-bearing in 3 places; replacing it risks breakage

questions.question: How do V1 state.json files on disk get upgraded?
questions.recommendation: Add migrate_discovery_state() pure function; wire into discovery_state() loader in Wave 2
questions.rationale: One live V1 blob exists (workflow-runner-hardening/state.json); transparent migration on read is safest

questions.question: What gets removed and when?
questions.recommendation: question_count and asked_count removed in Wave 4 only, after gate logic no longer depends on them
questions.rationale: Codex found Wave 2 too large if removals are bundled in; isolate cleanup to Wave 4

assumptions: run_factory.py must stay runnable at every wave boundary — no broken intermediate states
V1 state.json files will be migrated transparently on first read in Wave 2
complete flag stays in schema; unresolved[] is additive second gate, not a replacement
discovery.summary: I-7 adds answers{}, non_goals[], acceptance_criteria[], unresolved[] to discovery state. unresolved[] becomes an enforceable blocking gate at spec checkpoint. Delivered in 4 waves: (1) additive schema + migration fn, (2) migration wire-up + new CLI flags + test updates, (3) gate enforcement + status display, (4) cleanup question_count/asked_count + docs.
